name: Mead End
overview: Mead End is a small hamlet on the northern fringe of Bowerchalke parish in the Chalke Valley, south-west Wiltshire, about 9-11 miles from Salisbury and within the Cranborne Chase National Landscape. It sits at the source of the River Chalke, a classic chalk stream that flows north to join the River Ebble near Broad Chalke. Along with Misselfore and Woodminton it is one of the outlying hamlets of Bowerchalke, a sought-after village whose 13th-century Church of the Holy Trinity is the burial place of Nobel laureate William Golding, author of Lord of the Flies.
character: A tiny, tranquil chalk-stream hamlet of houses and farmland on the edge of a pretty downland village, deep in protected AONB countryside.
amenities: No shops or pub in the hamlet itself
Bowerchalke village (adjoining) has a Saturday village-hall market and a cricket club
Neighbouring Broad Chalke has the Queens Head pub, a community shop and tearoom, GP surgery and primary school
Superfast broadband available; main shopping in Salisbury

commutes.notes: About 9-11 miles down the Chalke Valley; Salisbury Reds bus runs through the area
From Salisbury, or from Tisbury station (~6 miles) on the same Exeter-Waterloo line

prices.summary: A very small hamlet of period and detached houses (Mead End House extends to over 2,300 sq ft, for example), within a Conservation Area that limits further development, so sales are infrequent and values sit high. Use the SP5 sold-price record for context.

thingsToDo: Walking and fly-fishing along the River Chalke chalk stream
The Bowerchalke Downs SSSI chalk grassland and Marleycombe Down (popular with paragliders)
The Chalke Valley History Festival each June
placesToEat: The Queens Head, Broad Chalke (~1-2 miles)
pros: Beautiful, secluded Chalke Valley and Cranborne Chase setting
At the source of a classic chalk stream with excellent walking
Strong community life in adjoining Bowerchalke and nearby Broad Chalke
cons: No amenities within the hamlet itself
Car-dependent and around 9-11 miles from Salisbury
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a peaceful chalk-stream hamlet in protected countryside, with village amenities a short drive away.

sources.label: VCH Wiltshire vol. 13: Bower Chalke - British History Online
